title: WoG · Daybreak – 1.4
question:
“For two months my aura had been one of the only real communication tools that I had, that didn’t require rounds of blinking and interpretation, or fumbling at a special keyboard…”

-is that supposed to be two years? Or did something
answer: Two months is intentional.

title: WoG · Daybreak – 1.5
question:
No, not necessarily. A master power that affects humans will affect capes unless said cape has a power that interferes with it. Case in point, Regent v Shadow Stalker and Khepri v everyone.

However Victoria’s aura (if active at the time)
answer: Don’t mix up the aura and the forcefield. Otherwise more or less correct.

title: WoG · Daybreak – 1.6
question: Did anyone else notice how Victoria referred to Dean as ‘her’ a couple of times? Looks like Amy’s changes are still intact.
answer: You’re misreading that. Take a second look at those parts.

title: WoG · Daybreak – 1.8
question:
So, the following is a comment that uses information from an author comment on Worm, so if you haven’t read Worm, or want to avoid getting outside of the story info, don’t read this.

-So… firstly, it’s a little bit ironic that Glory Girl
answer: You might be jumping to conclusions.

title: WoG · Daybreak – Interlude 1
question: Never read any of PRT Quest. What’s the connection to this chapter?
answer: PRT quest was set in Alaska – at one point the director sends some capes to deal with Prancer and Velvet

title: WoG · Flare – 2.4
question: … but how did the diplomatic incident get resolved?
answer: More on that in a bit.

title: WoG · Flare – 2.5
question:
The Yought Guard is a ridiculous concept in context of Worm that feels very much like a ret-con made during the PRT Quest after it had ended.

Its best to just consider any mention of it non-canon.
answer: Hate to break it to you, man, but it’s actual canon.

title: WoG · Flare – 2.4
question: How victims know their brain locked in space? Brain itself don’t have sensory nerve ending, don’t feel pain or and don’t have any have other sensory receptors inside (beside blood vessels). Pressure of locked area on blood vessels could
answer: They were screaming that. Intense headaches correlating in an serious way with movement & inability to move, neck pain that does much the same, hinging from a point above, leaky brainpan, a few of the affected were able to draw the correlation.

title: WoG · Flare – 2.5
question: I had that thought as well, further evidence for sharing a body is that Yamada had no reason to believe Byron wouldn’t show, yet there are still only eight chairs.
answer: Maybe Yamada planned to stand if Byron was there.

title: WoG · Flare – 2.5
question: … Can’t believe I am commenting on this, but I don’t think the children would resemble either of them. This actually raises an interesting question though, can Case 52s reproduce? I’d guess Sventa and Weld are sterile, but what about ones
answer: I would certainly hope a C52 wouldn’t reproduce. C53s on the other hand…

title: WoG · Flare – 2.7
question: What are “duffle coat toggles”?
answer: Horn shaped bits of plastic or bone (or whatever else) the size of a small finger, that you put through loops at the front.

title: WoG · Glare – 3.3
question:
Not exactly a typo, but I am having a very hard time following the conversation that starts with:

“It came up,” Rain said.

“Okay, good. But did you talk about why?”

I can’t tell who is saying what.
answer: I’ll get you started by clarifying that Rain said “It came up.”

title: WoG · Glare – 3.4
question: Tinkers are the most development-focused class. Their two bottlenecks are inspiration and materials, and unlike most capes, their midfight boosts aren’t just mostly-temporary powerboosts with sometimes-permanent upgrades. Quite the
answer: I’d say the two bottlenecks are more like time & materials. The power supplies the inspiration, much of the time.

title: WoG · Shadow – 5.11
question:
Hi, I’m new to commenting.

Correct me if I’m wrong but she didn’t say Ashley’s​ names here right?

“I’d like you to turn yourself in, and submit yourself into our custody,” I said.

Her eyes widened slightly.

“There it is,” Nailbiter
answer: End of prior chapter.

title: WoG · Torch – 7.6
question:
Unless it’s intentionally different from our world, the cerebellum is not the seat of perception in the brain.

The cerebellum is primarily involved with coordination and smooth movement, practiced movement in particular. Athletes like
answer:
I talked to an occupational therapist with a focus in head injuries – and it was described to me as the ‘small brain’ – not the seat of perception but a filter for perception, with a lot of localized injuries and issues in the cerebellum having some relationship with perception issues or disorders (schizophrenia, autism).

If there’s a hearsay or ‘that’s a

title: WoG · Eclipse – x.8
question:
So, there seems to be a plot hole here for me that maybe I didn’t understand correctly.

I was pretty sure that Ashley made a reference to the last time that Riley and her sat down for tea and how she wouldn’t allow that to happen again. I
answer: In Worm, Bonesaw’s interlude, Bonesaw/Riley have tea with a Damsel clone, drugging her, and then boil her alive, because it’s deemed an unworkable variant of Ashley.

title: WoG · Torch – 7.8
question:
Amy can use her power on herself, she did it to prevent bleeding when Siberian bit some of her fingers off.

She is (understandably) very reluctant to do so, though. It will take a long way to convince her to reach immortality.
answer: She didn’t use her power on herself – she used it on bacteria and such to create a ‘skin’ symbiote/organism that would stop the bleeding.

title: WoG · Torch – Interlude 7.x
question: An interesting bit about that particular memory is that it may have been poorly cooked (as indicated by hard-to-cut chicken). I think this dish was particularly horrible, because it was a Parmesan recipe. The smell of Parmesan cheese is
answer: Hard to cut cheese, not chicken.

title: WoG · Heavens – 12.x
question: The previous / next chapter is a little bugged, it’s before the last paragraph which may have been intentional or it’s accidentally extremely confusing.
answer: Intentional. It’s almost as if it’s placement was… warped.

title: WoG · Heavens – 12.x
question:
I’m laughing, omg. What was the thought process here?

The end of March will take place at the end of March, with a prank, just in time for April fools day????

This was the the most built up, elaborate prank that has ever been played on
answer: Was the plan for Vista to come back for this from before I had March stab her. There are seeds laid throughout. I thought it happening at the end of March was apropos.
